title: Rooftop rain catchment first-flush upgrade
summary: Separates the dirtiest first roof runoff and records roof risk before rainwater storage.
content: Rooftop rain catchment first-flush upgrade is a practical offline routine for rainwater collection. Inspect roof material, bird droppings, ash, dust, dead animals, chemicals, gutters, and nearby smoke before collecting. Discard or separate the first dirty runoff after dry periods. Screen leaves and insects before storage and cover containers against mosquitoes. Label rainwater as untreated unless proper safe-water steps and official guidance allow use. Stop collection after wildfire ash, chemical dust, industrial fallout, or unknown contamination. The goal is to make the action repeatable by a tired person, visible to helpers, and safe enough to stop when official help or a safer option appears.

warnings: Use Rooftop rain catchment first-flush upgrade as practical support, not as a replacement for official guidance, trained help, or evacuation when needed.

Filtering alone does not make chemically contaminated, fuel-contaminated, sewage-contaminated, or floodwater safe. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Rooftop rain catchment first-flush upgrade”.
